title: centaurea × donatiana
intro: Centaurea × donatiana, also known as the Bachelor's Button or Donats's Knapweed, is a hybrid plant species derived from the centaurea family, characterized by its vibrant blue flowers and fern-like foliage. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. Consuming Centaurea × donatiana is believed to provide various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, but it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for therapeutic purposes.
